posts.text: 'I couldn't breathe': South Korea's frenzied stock trading exposes margin loan risks

posts.author: dtyxg
posts.text: South Korea's Stock Market Deleveraging Storm: 1.2 Million Accounts Face Margin Calls, 360,000 Retail Investors Wiped Out — On July 16, South Korea’s benchmark KOSPI index plummeted 463.81 points, or 6.37%, to close at 6,820.60, once again breaching the psychological 7,000-point level. Just one day earlier, the index had surged 6.24%. This pattern of violent swings—plunge, rally, plunge again—has become the new normal for South Korean equities, as a full-scale deleveraging storm triggered by leveraged trading unfolds. Earlier the same day, the Bank of Korea raised its benchmark interest rate b…

posts.author: dtyxg
posts.text: South Korea's Stock Market Deleveraging Storm: 1.2 Million Accounts Face Margin Calls, 360,000 Retail Investors Wiped Out — https://finance.biggo.com/news/ffef6d30-c6de-4ba3-9a3b-72cf55e29dea Summary by AI (ChatGPT) What happened? More than 1.2 million leveraged trading accounts received margin calls . Around 320,000–360,000 accounts were forcibly liquidated after investors failed to meet margin requirements. Main trigger Retail investors borrowed heavily to buy Samsung Electronics , SK Hynix , and leveraged semiconductor ETFs during the AI rally. As semiconductor stocks declined , margin call…
